name: Error Handling and Retry Pattern
description: Demonstrates robust error handling in Power Automate using Scope actions, Run After settings, configurable retries, and structured error logging. A production-ready pattern for resilient flows.
key_features: Scope actions for grouping and error isolation
Run After settings to handle failures gracefully
Retry policy configuration on HTTP/API actions

flow_steps.action: Trigger: When an HTTP request is received
flow_steps.description: Accepts a JSON payload to process.
All main business logic lives inside this scope.

inner_actions.description: Makes an outbound HTTP call. Retry policy is configured here.
Writes the processed data to SharePoint.

flow_steps.description: This scope only runs if the Main Processing scope fails.

inner_actions.action: Create item in SharePoint - Error Log
inner_actions.description: Logs the error details to a dedicated error log list.

inner_actions.action: Send an email - Alert on Failure
inner_actions.subject: ALERT: Flow Failure - Error Handling Demo

flow_steps.description: Optional: runs cleanup or confirmation steps on success.

run_after_cheat_sheet.description: Configure 'Run After' on an action to control when it runs.

options.is successful: Run only if the previous action succeeded (default)
options.has failed: Run only if the previous action failed
options.is skipped: Run only if the previous action was skipped
options.has timed out: Run only if the previous action timed out

run_after_cheat_sheet.tip: You can select multiple options (e.g., failed + timed out) to handle multiple error states.

retry_policy_types.none: No retries. Fails immediately on error.
retry_policy_types.fixed: Retries a fixed number of times with a fixed interval.
retry_policy_types.exponential: Retries with exponentially increasing wait times. Best for transient errors.

notes: Replace {{OPS_TEAM_EMAIL}} with your operations team's email address.
The Scope action is the key building block for error handling in Power Automate.
Always set Run After on your error handler scope to catch Failed, TimedOut, and Skipped.
Exponential retry is preferred for HTTP/API calls to handle transient network issues.
Log errors to a persistent store (SharePoint, Dataverse, Azure Table) for auditability.
